subject: UX & Research
title: Flash: Rise and Fall
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Flash: Rise and Fall.

hook.text: In the early 2000s, Flash made the web dance. Animations, games, and video ran in a browser plugin. It felt like magic.

buildup.text: Flash designers created splash pages, animated intros, and interactive experiences impossible with plain HTML.

discovery.text: At its peak, Flash was installed on 98% of desktop browsers. YouTube originally ran entirely on Flash video.

twist.text: Steve Jobs refused Flash on the iPhone in 2010. He called it slow, insecure, and battery-draining. The web moved on.

climax.text: HTML5 replaced Flash. Open standards beat proprietary plugins. Adobe killed Flash entirely in December 2020.

punchline.text: Flash made the web creative. Open standards made it last. Proprietary dies.

quiz.question: What ultimately killed Flash as a web technology?
options: Apple blocking it from the iPhone and HTML5 replacing its capabilities with open standards
Flash became too expensive for developers
Users stopped wanting animated content on the web
